channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: WATCH: Two people reportedly tasered by police at Marjorie Taylor Green town hall, 3 arrested
item.description: Multiple protesters were forcibly escorted out of a town hall event in Georgia hosted by GOP Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene, including two who were tasered by police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IRS agent who investigated Hunter Biden expected to be named IRS acting chief
item.description: An IRS whistleblower who testified publicly about investigations into Hunter Biden’s taxes is expected to be promoted to IRS acting commissioner, two sources familiar with the plan tell The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden says Trump administration has done 'breathtaking' damage to Social Security
item.description: Former President Joe Biden warned a Chicago audience assembled Tuesday at the national conference of Advocates, Counselors and Representatives for the Disabled that the Trump administration has "taken a hatchet" to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Army takes control of federal land along New Mexico border to increase security, protect the environment
item.description: Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum announced the U.S. Army will take control of federal lands along the U.S.-Mexico border in order to increase security and protect the environment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge orders federal agencies to release billions of dollars from two Biden-era initiatives
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday ordered the Trump administration to release billions of dollars meant to finance climate and infrastructure projects across the country. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department can cut funding for legal guidance for people facing deportation, US judge says
item.description: A federal judge has allowed the Justice Department to temporarily stop funding legal education programs for people facing deportation or immigration court while a lawsuit brought by the organizations that provide the service moves forward in court.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Boone And Cedar Attanasio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AOC claims Republicans will 'trick' Americans into identity politics, stoking racial divisions
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez told rally goers Monday evening not to let Republicans "trick" them into thinking they are defined by their race or other immutable characteristics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP hits back after judges demand Trump allies be condemned for targeting judiciary
item.description: Sen. Cynthia Lummis led a letter from Wyoming's congressional delegation slamming condemnations from more than 100 Cowboy State jurists for inaction against Trump's "attacks" on judges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bipartisan senators rebuke White House move to end legal aid for unaccompanied minors
item.description: The letter obtained by NPR marks a rare bipartisan critique from Capitol Hill of the administration's immigration policy.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Jeffries: Court should hold Trump officials in contempt over wrongly deported Maryland man
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) said Tuesday that the Supreme Court should hold members of the Trump administration in contempt of court if they don’t move to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported last month to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump floats deporting 'homegrown criminals' to El Salvador
item.description: Trump has suggested shipping off U.S. citizens who commit violent crimes to places like El Salvador's notorious mega-prison CECOT.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: 281 bills fall victim to deadline at Nevada Legislature. Here are the highlights
item.description: A total of 281 bills missed a Friday deadline to win committee passage in Carson City, the first major deadline at the Nevada Legislature this session. If that seems like a lot, consider that 875 bills are still alive.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What to know as Social Security anti-fraud measures take effect
item.description: Individuals who are flagged will be required to prove their identity in person for their Social Security claim to be processed.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump hosts El Salvador's Nayib Bukele amid deportation controversy
item.description: Since March, El Salvador has accepted more than 200 Venezuelan immigrants accused by the U.S. of gang activity and violent crimes.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Bill de Blasio on what's next for the Democratic Party
item.description: De Blasio, who has received fierce criticism from President Trump this week, believes the Democrats blew the presidential election on multiple fronts.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's commerce secretary says new electronics tariff exemptions are temporary, chip tariffs coming
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Tariff exemptions announced Friday on electronics like smartphones and laptops are only a temporary reprieve until the Trump administration develops a new tariff approach specific to the semiconductor industry, U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Senate Minority Leader Johnson is optimistic lawmakers will make session deadline
item.description: State lawmakers have a big hill to climb with just over a month before the Legislative session is over. Despite economic uncertainty, Senate Minority Leader Mark Johnson, R-East Grand Forks, said he’s optimistic they’ll get a budget passed before the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Lukas Levin, and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump urges Congress to make daylight saving time permanent
item.description: President Donald Trump said Congress should “push hard for more Daylight at the end of a day” in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: WorldPride organizers caution transgender visitors from abroad
item.description: (The Hill) - Organizers of WorldPride, a global LGBTQ Pride event series to be held this year in Washington, issued an advisory Friday for transgender people from overseas, citing recent policies put forward by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Dan Caine, the nation's newly-confirmed top military adviser
item.description: The Senate voted around 2 a.m. Friday to confirm retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine as chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. Trump plucked him from retirement to be his top military adviser.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, Juliana Kim, and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Beijing slaps 125% tariffs on U.S. goods in latest U.S.-China trade escalation
item.description: China signals the latest tariff hike will be its last round of tit-for-tat measures, prompting sharp falls in European shares, as Asian stocks end the day mixed.
item.dc:creator: Sherisse Pham, MPR News

item.title: Trump threatens to put higher tariff rate back if countries don’t strike deals
item.description: President Trump on Thursday threatened to reverse course on a 90-day pause on country-specific tariffs if countries don’t strike a deal with his administration before then.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5
